ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for smoky BBQ beef brisket soup meal; closest_parent 'beef soup food product' (FOODON_00002473) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Beef meat soups and broths) is the closest parent; no exact code exists for a BBQ-flavoured beef brisket soup meal with bread.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept found for this composite meal product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a bowl of smoky BBQ beef brisket soup (approximately 350 g) and one portion of bread (approximately 100 g), as served at Subway Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Beef brisket, cooked; Beef broth; BBQ sauce; White bread (composite estimation)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Smoky BBQ Beef Brisket Soup Meal exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries: cooked beef brisket (FDC ID 174032), canned beef broth (FDC ID 171534), BBQ sauce (FDC ID 172235), and enriched white bread (FDC ID 325871). Approximate component ratio per 100 g of meal: beef brisket 35%, broth 45%, BBQ sauce 8%, bread 12%. Values represent a plausible central estimate for this meal category. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — beef stew with vegetables and bread analogous estimate; white bread GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Smoky BBQ Beef Brisket Soup Meal. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on analogous composite: beef-based soup with bread. The dominant glycemic contributor is the bread component (white bread GI ~71, wholegrain ~50); the soup broth and beef brisket contribute negligible glycemic load. Weighted composite GI estimated at ~52 reflecting a mixed meal with moderate bread proportion. GL per serving = (52 × 42.75 g available carbohydrate per 450 g serving) / 100 = 22.3. Analogous food basis: 'Beef stew with vegetables' and 'white bread' from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~2160 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to its very high sodium content (~2160 mg per 450 g serving), which represents approximately 94% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; portion reduction and requesting low-sodium broth are advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~22.3 per serving) driven by bread component and BBQ sauce sugars
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this meal due to its moderate glycemic load of approximately 22.3 per serving, primarily from the bread and BBQ sauce; choosing wholegrain bread options and reducing sauce quantity can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this meal infrequently due to its high sodium content (~2160 mg per serving) and moderate saturated fat (~8.1 g per serving), both of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk; opting for smaller portions and leaner preparation methods is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Smoky BBQ Beef Brisket Soup Meal is a Western-style hot meal served at Subway Singapore and select Singapore food outlets, consisting of a hearty bowl of slow-cooked, BBQ-seasoned beef brisket in a rich, smoky beef broth, accompanied by bread (subject to availability). The beef brisket is typically braised or slow-cooked until tender, then finished with a smoky BBQ sauce that imparts a sweet, tangy, and smoky flavour profile. The broth is deep and savoury, enriched by the rendered collagen and fat from the brisket. The bread component — which may vary by outlet — adds a starchy, mildly sweet counterpoint and serves as the primary glycemic contributor of the meal. Per 100 g, the meal provides approximately 118 kcal, 9.2 g protein, 4.8 g fat, and 9.5 g carbohydrate, making it a moderate-energy, protein-rich option. The sodium content is notably high (~480 mg per 100 g), which is a primary clinical concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per serving is moderate (~22.3), driven largely by the bread and BBQ sauce sugars. This meal is suitable for non-vegetarian, non-vegan consumers; halal status depends on the specific outlet and sourcing. It is not gluten-free due to the bread component. Clinically, the meal is best consumed in moderation by individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease, with attention to bread choice and sauce quantity.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Smoky BBQ Beef Brisket Soup Meal was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional source. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked beef brisket, beef broth, BBQ sauce, and white bread. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for beef-based soups and white bread. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation and absence of a direct source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central component entries (cooked beef brisket FDC 174032, beef broth FDC 171534, BBQ sauce FDC 172235, white bread FDC 325871); no exact branded product entry was available. 2. Bread type is variable per outlet (described as 'subject to availability' in the source record); values assume standard white bread as the default. 3. Halal status is not confirmed at the record level; individual outlet certification should be verified. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA composite source entries used for this estimation. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported with sufficient confidence across the composite source entries. 6. GI set to 52 (low) as an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for this specific product is not available. 7. Sodium content is notably high and is the primary clinical concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. 8. The meal is served at Subway Funan and Funan Mix & Match outlets on field survey data at SGD 17.00.
